food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — submarine sandwich with fried chicken nuggets side

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined submarine sandwich and chicken nuggets combo meal. FOODON_00002473 (submarine sandwich) is used as the closest parent for the sandwich component. The nuggets side and meal format are not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwich) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a Subway-branded combo meal with chicken nuggets in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a fast food combo meal of this type.

main_ingredients: processed turkey and ham (Subway Melt filling)
salad vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, capsicum)

serving.description: 1 meal (6-inch Subway Melt sub + 5 chicken nuggets)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 6-inch Subway Melt submarine sandwich (approximately 250 g) paired with 5 breaded chicken nuggets (approximately 85–90 g), totalling roughly 420 g for the complete meal.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Melt 6-inch and Chicken Nuggets — USDA Branded Food / Subway Nutrition Data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) — Subway LLC published nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy component values
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for the complete Subway Melt 5-Piece Nuggets Meal as sold in Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway 6-inch Melt sub (FDC ID 2103965, approximately 232 g, ~430 kcal per sub) and (2) USDA Branded Food entry for Subway chicken nuggets 5-piece (FDC ID 2104012, approximately 85 g, ~230 kcal). Per-100g values are derived from the combined meal weight of approximately 420 g and combined nutrient totals. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual Singapore-market values may differ slightly due to local ingredient sourcing.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based mixed meals — analogous estimate from white bread sub and breaded chicken components
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Melt 5-Piece Nuggets Meal. GI is estimated at 56 (medium) based on: (1) white wheat bread submarine roll GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); (2) breaded fried chicken nuggets GI ~35–40 (low, protein and fat dominant); (3) combined meal GI estimated using carbohydrate-weighted averaging across components. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (56 × 70.6 g carbohydrate in 420 g serving) / 100 ≈ 15.8. This is a medium GL. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2016 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal as a single serving provides approximately 84% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from processed meats, cheese, sauces, and the bread roll.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~56) and moderate glycemic load (~15.8 per meal) from refined wheat bread roll
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its medium GI wheat bread base; pairing with high-fibre vegetables and avoiding sugary beverages in the combo can help moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from processed meats and cheese, and trans fat from deep-fried nuggets
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is not recommended for frequent consumption by individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium load (~2016 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed deli meats and cheese, and potential trans fat from deep-frying of the nuggets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Melt 5-Piece Nuggets Meal is a fast food combo meal sold at Subway outlets in Singapore, consisting of a 6-inch Subway Melt submarine sandwich paired with five breaded and deep-fried chicken nuggets. The Subway Melt sub features a toasted Italian or white wheat bread roll filled with layers of processed turkey, ham, and bacon, topped with melted cheese and a selection of fresh salad vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, and capsicum, finished with condiments such as mayonnaise or mustard. The chicken nuggets are breaded, seasoned, and deep-fried, providing a crispy exterior with a tender chicken interior. The meal is classified as ultra-processed due to the use of processed deli meats, commercial bread, and industrially produced nuggets. Nutritionally, the meal is moderate in energy (~660 kcal per serving), moderate in protein (~40 g per serving), and notable for its high sodium content (~2016 mg per serving), which represents a significant proportion of the daily recommended limit. The meal has a medium glycemic index (~56) driven by the refined wheat bread roll, and a medium glycemic load (~15.8 per serving). It contains saturated fat from cheese and processed meats, and small amounts of trans fat from the deep-fried nuggets. This meal is widely available at Subway franchise locations across Singapore and is popular as a lunch or dinner option. Clinically, it is of concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its very high sodium content, and requires portion awareness for those with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Subway Melt 5 nuggets meal?
Is Subway Melt good for diabetics?
Subway Melt 5pc nuggets set meal calories

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for the complete Subway Melt 5-Piece Nuggets Meal as sold in Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA Branded Food entries for the Subway 6-inch Melt sub and Subway chicken nuggets (5-piece), which are the closest available approved-source matches. GI is an analogous estimate using carbohydrate-weighted averaging from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for white bread and breaded chicken. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA Branded Food data is specific to Subway products, reducing estimation uncertainty.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA Branded Food entries for Subway 6-inch Melt (FDC ID 2103965) and Subway 5-piece chicken nuggets (FDC ID 2104012), combined and expressed per 100 g of the total meal weight (~420 g). Singapore-market formulations may differ slightly. 2. Halal status: Subway Singapore operates as a non-halal chain; the meal is not certified halal. is_halal is set to false. 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries for these Subway products. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries for these Subway products. 5. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries for these Subway products. 6. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific meal exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The meal is sold at SGD 15.50 per field survey data data (price_min_sgd and price_max_sgd both 15.50). 8. This record covers the field survey data listing as served at Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match outlets.
